title: Seeking Wisdom: From Darwin to Munger
subtitle: A multidisciplinary exploration of how our thoughts are influenced, why we make misjudgments, and the tools to think better — drawn from Darwin, Munger, Einstein, Franklin, and the world's clearest thinkers.
author: Peter Bevelin
themes: The pursuit of wisdom as a lifelong discipline
Understanding human nature to avoid misjudgments
Learning from the best thinkers across disciplines
Objectivity and self-criticism as the highest virtues
Darwinian thinking: disconfirming your own beliefs
excerpt: A masterful compendium of wisdom from Darwin, Munger, Einstein, and Franklin — organized as a practical decision-making manual for anyone who wants to avoid costly mistakes. Bevelin asks: what can we learn from the clearest thinkers in history about how to think clearly ourselves?
